steps.title: Set up the average rate over a 10-minute interval
steps.content: $$\text{Average rate} = \frac{T(\text{end}) - T(\text{start})}{10\text{ min}}.$$ The greatest decrease corresponds to the most negative value (largest drop in temperature).

steps.title: Read the temperatures at each endpoint from the graph
steps.content:
From the scatterplot:
  • $t=0$ min: about $195^{\circ}\text{F}$
  • $t=10$ min: about $155^{\circ}\text{F}$
  • $t=30$ min: about $120^{\circ}\text{F}$
  • $t=40$ min: about $105^{\circ}\text{F}$
  • $t=50$ min: about $100^{\circ}\text{F}$
  • $t=60$ min: about $90^{\circ}\text{F}$
  • $t=90$ min: about $80^{\circ}\text{F}$
  • $t=100$ min: about $75^{\circ}\text{F}$

steps.title: Compute each interval's average rate and pick the largest drop
steps.content:
Choice A (0–10): $\frac{155-195}{10} = -4.0^{\circ}\text{F/min}$
Choice B (30–40): $\frac{105-120}{10} = -1.5^{\circ}\text{F/min}$
Choice C (50–60): $\frac{90-100}{10} = -1.0^{\circ}\text{F/min}$
Choice D (90–100): $\frac{75-80}{10} = -0.5^{\circ}\text{F/min}$

The most negative rate is $-4.0^{\circ}\text{F/min}$ on $[0, 10]$. That confirms A is correct.
